_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to design and test algorithms that identify congruent or similar shapes.

_source.w: We are learning to design and test algorithms that identify congruent or similar shapes.
y: Creating algorithms to identify congruence and similarity connects geometric reasoning with computational thinking.
Testing algorithms against varied examples develops our debugging skills and geometric knowledge simultaneously.
These algorithms have real applications in computer vision, pattern recognition and quality control.
eg: Designing a flowchart that tests whether two triangles are congruent using SSS, SAS, ASA or RHS.
Writing pseudocode to check if two shapes are similar by comparing corresponding angles and side ratios.
Testing the algorithm with pairs of shapes — some congruent, some similar, some neither.
Debugging an algorithm that incorrectly identifies non-congruent shapes as congruent.
Extending the algorithm to work with quadrilaterals.
Evaluating the efficiency of different algorithmic approaches to the same classification problem.
